ASBESTOS MOTION CALENDAR ON NOV-02-2017 IN DEPT. 503, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO MAINTAIN THE TRIAL DATE WITH PREFERENCE IS GRANTED. THE OCTOBER 2, 2017 TRIAL DATE IS MAINTAINED WITH PREFERENCE STATUS PURSUANT TO C.C.P. 36(A). THE NOVEMBER 20, 2017 RETURN HOLDING DATE IS VACATED. THE NEW RETURN HOLDING DATE IS FEBRUARY 20, 2018 AT 11:15A.M. IN DEPARTMENT 503. LAST DAY PURSUANT TO C.C.P. 36(F) IS MARCH 2, 2018. SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE IN ROOM 603 AT 9:30A.M. ON FEBRUARY 15, 2018. THERE WILL BE A MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE WITH THE COURT PURSUANT TO CRC, RULE 3.1380 AND SAN FRANCISCO LOCAL RULES OF COURT, RULES 5.0 AND 20.2 ON FEBRUARY 20, 2018 AT 1:30P.M. IN DEPARTMENT 503. THE PARTIES SHALL COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULES 5.0 AND 20.2 AND PROVIDE A CONFIDENTIAL SETTLEMENT STATEMENT BY FEBRUARY 15, 2018 AT 2:00P.M. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: CYNTHIA MING-MEI LEE, MATTER NOT REPORTED. (503)

Notice Of Continued Motion To Consolidate For Trial The Cases Against Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc. And Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc. Formerly Known As Kaiser Cement Corporation
Off Calendar Feb-19-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Andrew Chew of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff.
Andrew Sharp of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9500), appearing for defendants Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc. and Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc.
CJ Manoli of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9500), appearing for defendants Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc. and Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc.
Laura Przetak of SPANOS | PRZETAK (510-250-0200), Designated Defense Counsel.

RULING: PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE FOR TRIAL THE CASES AGAINST KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC. AND HANSON PERMANENTE CEMENT, INC. FORMERLY KNOWN AS KAISER CEMENT CORPORATION
Parties stipulated to have all matters heard simultaneously. Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion to consolidate for trial the cases against Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc. and Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc. formerly known as Kaiser Cement corporation, into the following subgroups: 1) wrongful death/mesothelioma; 2) living/lung cancer: 3) wrongful death/lung cancer; 4) living/other cancer; 5) wrongful death/other cancer; 6) living/asbestosis; 7) wrongful death/asbestosis; 8) living/pleural disease, is denied. The actions do not involve sufficient common questions of law or fact, economy and convenience would not be served by a joint trial, and consolidation may prejudice one or both of the defendants. Prevailing party is to submit a proposed order in compliance with C.R.C. 3.1312. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Reporter: Sheila Pham, CSR #13293 spham28@yahoo.com (Tel: 415-517-5439) (503/CML)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Maintaining The Current Trial Date With Preference
Off Calendar Nov-02-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / moving party Pat Stewart

RULING:
PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER MAINTAINING THE CURRENT TRIAL DATE WITH PREFERENCE

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion to maintain the trial date with preference is granted. No opposition filed. The October 2, 2017 trial date is maintained with preference status pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). The November 20, 2017 return holding date is vacated. The new return holding date is February 20, 2018 at 11:15a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is March 2, 2018. All pre-trial dates and deadlines remain dictated by the October 2, 2017 trial date, which has passed. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Pang Ly by February 13, 2018 at 12:00p.m. Settlement conference in room 603 at 9:30a.m. on February 15, 2018. There will be a mandatory settlement conference with the Court pursuant to CRC, Rule 3.1380 and San Francisco Local Rules of Court, Rules 5.0 and 20.2 on February 20, 2018 at 1:30p.m. in Department 503. The parties shall comply with Local Rules 5.0 and 20.2 and provide a confidential settlement statement by February 15, 2018 at 2:00p.m. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, matter not reported (503/CML)
